ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: ni ame̱tšṍmo̱ kanei ye̱ ṅwei atatu le̱ mli, ní ame̱tšo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱. Ni efe nakai.
translated: If someone gives you three pieces of bread in the morning, and someone else gives you a basket in the afternoon. That is how it is.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: And Adam said: The woman whom you gave to be with me, she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Ni ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱nyie ya-yai, nū le̱ ke̱ eṅã le̱, ni ame̱hĩe gboo he.
translated: If this person is truly your friend, tell them the truth, and do not hide anything from them.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ Adam ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Nẽgbe̱ oyo̱?
translated: Then Jehovah God called to Adam and said to him: Where are you?

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Ni mike̱ nyē̱ aaṅmẽ oke̱ yō le̱ teṅ, ke̱ oseši ke̱ *eseši teṅ; †eetſa oyitšo nõ, ni **bo hũ ootſa enanetšitši nõ. *Gal.
translated: If anyone thinks they are something when they are nothing, they deceive themselves; each one should test their own actions, then they can take pride in themselves alone, without comparing themselves to someone else. *Gal.

translated: When Kain brought his offering, it was not accepted. That is why Kain was angry, and his face fell.

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Ni ekē̱: Meni ofe nē̱? Onyemi le̱ *lá gbē mīblo̱ mītšé̱mi ke̱-mīdše̱ šikpoṅ sũ le̱ mli! *Mat.
translated: And he said: What do you want? She replied, "Let my two sons sit at your right and your left in your kingdom!" Matt.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Ni nni le̱ damo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, gbĩĩ oha ke̱ nyoṅmai-enumo̱.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Ni mahã mikpãṅmo̱ ní mike̱nye ekpãṅ le̱ amã ši, ni nu afua le̱ ebaplenẽṅ helo fẽ doṅṅ, ni asaṅ nu afua ko ebakpãtãṅ šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe gbĩkogbĩko.
translated: If you do not wash your hands well before eating, you may get diseases from the food, and sometimes you may get stomach problems that make you feel pain often.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Miṅõ mikwãbue̱te̱ mito atatui le̱ amli, ni efe kpāṅmo̱ ni yo̱ mike̱ šikpoṅ le̱ teṅ le̱ he okadi.
translated: When I woke up this morning, I saw that the sky was dark and it looked like it would rain today.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ši Šem ke̱ Iafet ko̱ mama ni ame̱ṅõ-fõ ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kõdši ano̱, ni ame̱nyie se̱se̱, ni ame̱te ni ame̱ke̱-yaha ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ; ši ame̱tšṍmo̱ ame̱hĩeaṅ ame̱hã se̱gbe̱, ni ame̱naa ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ.
translated: These are the descendants of Shem, Ham, and Japheth, the sons of Noah, who had children after the flood. The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Mene̱mei amli mei dše̱ ſã dšeṅmādši le̱ aṅšo̱kpo̱i le̱ ano̱, ye̱ ame̱šikpõdši le̱ ano̱, mofẽmo ke̱ ewiemo̱, ye̱ ame̱kutšei ke̱ ame̱dšeṅmādši le̱ anā. Zak.
translated: Some people say that if you do good, good things will come to you, but if you do bad, bad things will come to you, and that is how life is, and people should always do good. End.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa bii le̱ awekui ye̱ ame̱sešibii le̱ anã ye̱ ame̱mãdši le̱ amli; ni mene̱mei amli dšeṅmãdši le̱ dše̱ fã šikpoṅ le̱ ṅo̱ ye̱ nu afua le̱ se̱.
translated: Just as Noah entered the ark with his family and all the animals with him; so also we must enter into the presence of God with our families and all that we have.

translated: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the Lord appeared to him and said, "I am God Almighty; walk before me faithfully and be blameless."

translated: Abram moved his tent and went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar to the Lord.

text: Ni Abram kē̱ Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ ake̱: Mīwó minine no̱ mītšõ Iehowa ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱,

translated: This is what Jehovah the Lord says: Ask me about things to come! But why do you question me about my children or the work of my hands?

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.

translated: And God said to Abraham: Do not be afraid, for I am your shield, your very great reward.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ni ame̱hã ame̱tše̱ wein enu nakai nyō le̱ṅ; ni onukpa le̱ yiṅ te, ni eke̱ etše̱ yawo̱; ni elee ešikãmo̱ mli lo ešitē mli.
translated: If someone slaps you on one cheek, turn the other to them also; if someone takes your coat, do not withhold your shirt from them. Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what belongs to you, do not demand it back.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”

translated: Then God came to Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him: Look, you are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.

translated: When God speaks to someone, and the person hears; and they understand, and they do what is right, then there is peace in their life.

translated: If you believe that God created everything, then you should also know that He is able to change anything; so do not be afraid, because nothing is too difficult for Him to do for you.

translated: If you hear this, think about it: Why do some people always want to be first everywhere, but when it comes to work, they want others to go before them?

translated: These are the children of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Keran.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Ši na, wo̱mĩfĩmo̱ ṅmā̃kei ye̱ ṅmõši, ni na, mike le̱ te ši damo̱ ši, ni na, nyekei le̱ babo̱le mike le̱ ni ame̱kotole̱.
translated: If so, you will know what is good, and if not, you will not do what is good, and if you do not, others will not be happy with you.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Ni enyemimei le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Aso maṅtše̱ po okē̱ ooye ye̱ wo̱no̱ ne̱? alo ye obaye wo̱no̱ ne̱? Ni ame̱nye̱le̱ fè tšutšu le̱ ye̱ elamõi le̱ ke̱ ewiemo̱i le̱ ahewo̱.
translated: And my question is this: Did you see the person who came here? Or did someone else come here? The person who knows should tell us so that we will know what really happened.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Iosef še̱ enyemimei le̱ aṅõ le̱, ame̱kplo̱ etade ni dši atade wuyã fe̱fe̱o ni ewo le̱ ye̱ ehe, K.
translated: When he arrived there, because Joseph's brothers did not recognize him, they treated him like a stranger and spoke harshly to him.

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Nyebaä ni nyehãa wo̱hõale̱ wo̱hãa Išmaelbii le̱, ši nyekahāa wo̱ke̱ wo̱niné mïsa ehe; edšake̱ wo̱nyemi ke̱ wo̱belo dšilo̱. Ni enyemimei le̱ kplẽ.
translated: But even though you are descendants of Ishmael, if you do not accept the message, you will not be part of the promise. This is the truth.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda kē̱ ešāyō Tamar ake̱: Yahĩ ši ake̱ okũlafo ye̱ otše̱ we le̱ ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni mibi Šela lē̱ aada. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni le̱ hũ ekabagbo tamo̱ enyemimei le̱. Ni Tamar te eyahĩ etše̱ we le̱.
translated: And Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law: Remain a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up. For he thought: He might die like his brothers. So Tamar went and stayed in her father’s house.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eli dše̱ibii ke̱ ake̱: Nẽgbe̱ yō adſamaṅ ní tá Enaim gbe̱ le̱ toi le̱ yo̱? Ni ame̱ke: Yō adſamaṅ ko be̱ bie̱ ne̱.
translated: This is what happened that day: Why did the woman go to Enaim by the road? The answer: The woman went to see something.

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni esu enine le̱ ekoṅ le̱, na, enyemi le̱ ba; ni ekē̱: Mẽba otšere̱ mll ke̱-ba ne̱? Ni awole̱ Perez. Mat.
translated: While she was giving birth, one of them put out his hand, so the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his wrist and said, "This one came out first." But he drew back his hand, and his brother came out, and she said, "So this is how you have broken out!" And he was named Perez. Matt.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth. And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom.

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni miwó migbē no̱ ni mibo le̱, ešĩ etade le̱ ye̱ miṅõ, ni edšo foi.
translated: If you are in trouble, those you call your friends will leave you, only your family will stay with you, and help you out.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ešišitšõmo̱ ne̱; nidši etẽ le̱ gbĩĩ etẽ ni;
translated: But Joseph said: Don’t be afraid; am I in the place of God?

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ši abološãlo̱i anukpa le̱, ese̱ṅle̱, take̱ be̱ni Iosef tšõame̱ šiši le̱.
translated: If you are hungry, come, sit, and eat what Joseph has cooked.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, bo̱ni edšadše šiši etšõwo̱ le̱, nakai eba mli; mi le̱ aṅõmi ato migbe̱he ekoṅ, ni le̱ le̱ ase̱ṅle̱.
translated: If you are in trouble, and your friends do not help you, do not worry; just remember that you have yourself, and that is enough.

ref: You are now governor of all Egypt!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Na, miṅõo mito Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱. Bo̱f.
translated: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: Look, I have put you in charge of the whole land of Egypt. Go.

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Ni Farao wo Iosef gbe̱i ake̱ Zafenat-Panea; ni eṅõ On oso̱fo Potifera biyō Asenat le̱ ehãle̱ gblã. Ni Iosef dše kpo eyakpa Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: Then Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-Paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, as a wife. And Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt.

ref: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef bua able nā eto babaō dientše̱ take̱ ṅšo̱ nā šia, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni efõ kanemo̱; edšake̱ ebe̱ yikule̱.
translated: When Joseph heard what his father said about the dream, he kept it in his mind; but his brothers were jealous of him.

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Ši Iosef nyemi Benyamin le̱, Iakob hãã eke̱ enyemimei le̱ aya; edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni aleenõ ošrã ko akayašrãle̱.
translated: When Joseph saw Benjamin, Jacob’s own son, he asked: Is this your youngest brother you told me about?

ref: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
text: Ni etšṍ ehe ke̱-dše ame̱ṅõ ni eyafó; ni ekũ ese̱ eba ame̱ṅõ ekoṅ ni ekeame̱ bawie; ni edšie Simeon ye̱ ame̱teṅ, ni efĩle ye̱ ame̱hĩe.
translated: And this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him; and it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

ref: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
text: Ni wo̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, wo̱dšee sralo̱i;
translated: If you do this: The elders say, you will have peace;

ref: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
text: Ni eha mli ake̱, beni ame̱dšie ame̱kotokui le̱ amli nii le̱, na, mofẽmo šika ni afĩ le̱ ye̱ ekotoku le̱ mli. Ni beni ame̱na ame̱sika ni afĩmo̱fĩmo̱i le̱, ame̱ke̱ ame̱tše̱ fẽ še gbeyei.
translated: If you are in a place, and you see someone doing something wrong there, and you keep quiet and do not speak out, you are also part of those doing the wrong thing. But if you speak out against the wrongdoing, you are not part of those doing wrong.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: And their father Jacob said to them: You have made me childless: Joseph is gone, Simeon is gone, and now you want to take Benjamin; everything is against me!

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: Ni ebiame̱ bo̱ni ame̱yo̱, ni ekē̱: Ani nyetše̱ nūmo le̱ *ni nyetšĩ etā le̱ ye̱ dšogbāṅ lo? Ani eye̱ dšeṅ lolo? *
translated: If someone asks you, say: I have heard what you said *or I have not heard what you said. Is that what you want?*

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
text: Ni efo̱ ehĩe ni edše kpo, ni emĩa ehĩe, ni ekē̱: Nyeke̱ niyenii abamãmo̱a ši!
translated: When you wake up in the morning, in the afternoon, and at night: Always remember to pray!

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Ni ebṍi taomo̱ ke̱-dše̱ onukpa le̱ no̱ ni eyagbe nā ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ no̱; ni ayana kpulu le̱ ye̱ Benyamin kotoku le̱ mli.
translated: And the man did as he was told and spent the night there; and they prepared a place for him in Benjamin’s house.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Ni anu sane ne̱ ye̱ Farao we le̱ ake̱: Iosef nyemimei eba! Ni Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ná be mīše̱.
translated: This is what Pharaoh said to them: Go to Joseph! And Pharaoh said to all the people, Do whatever he tells you.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Kē̱mo̱ onyemimei le̱ ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyetẽrea nyekoloi le̱ adšatšui, ni nyeyiaṅ ke̱-yaä Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱,
translated: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: This is what I command you: Take wagons from Egypt, go and bring your father and your families, and come to the land of Canaan.

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Ni bo le̱ afā̃o ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa ſīlii ke̱-dše̱a Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱ nyebāa nyebii ke̱ nyeṅãmei, ni nyeyaṅõa nyetše̱ ke̱-baä.
translated: This is what you must say: The Lord says: I brought Israel out of Egypt, out of the land where they were slaves, but they have turned away from me.

ref: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
text: Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe; šikpoṅ le̱, beni hĩ fè fẽ ni dši Gošen šikpoṅ le̱, hã otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ ahĩ dše̱i; ni ke̱dši ole ame̱teṅ meikomei ṅí dši gbomei ni sa le̱, no le̱ ṅõame̱ ofe oṅukpai ye̱ mikoloi le̱ ano̱.
translated: Mizraim was in mourning for his son; he was mourning, but his son and all those who lived in Goshen were not mourning, and he gave his people food to eat; and he did not allow any stranger to enter their land, so that no one would bring any trouble to them.

ref: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
text: Ni Iosef ké̱: Ni nyešika etã ne̱, nyeṅõa nyekoloi nyehãa, ni mahãnye ye̱ nyekoloi le̱ anā.
translated: And Joseph said: I am going out, I will take my car, if you want, you can take the car too.

ref: Then Joseph made his sons move away from Jacob's knees, and Joseph bowed down in front of him with his face to the ground.
text: Ni Iosef dšieame̱ ke̱-dše̱ enakutšei ateṅ ke̱-ba, ni eho ekoto ši, eṅõ ehĩe ebu ši.
translated: When Joseph arrived where his brothers were, they took off his robe, the beautiful robe he was wearing.

ref: You will tie your donkey to a choice grapevine and wash your clothes in wine from those grapes.
text: Eefĩ etedšibi eṅmõ weintšo ke̱ etedšiyō bi hũ eṅmõ weintšonine; eke̱ wein aafo̱ etade he, ni ehebo le̱ eke̱ weinyibii amli lá aafo̱ he. Ioel
translated: If the day of the Lord comes, who will be able to stand; for it will be a great and terrible day, and who can endure it. Joel

ref: Joseph, you are a fruitful vine growing near a stream and climbing a wall.
text: Iosef le̱, tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, nu ni baä he tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, ni enidši le̱ kwō̱lo̱ö̱ gbogbo. Hos.
translated: Joseph said, "Take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were trying to take the child's life are dead." Hos.

ref: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
text: Abō̱ le̱ ke̱ te̱kplo ni yo̱ mli le̱ ahe ye̱ Hetbii le̱ adeṅ.
translated: The goat is in the room and the sheep is outside.

ref: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
text: Ni beni efómo̱ gbĩĩ le̱ ebo le̱, Iosef ke̱ Farao we le̱ bii le̱ wie ni ekē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mihĩe eba nyam ye̱ nyehĩe le̱, mīkpanye fai, nyewoa Farao toi no̱ ake.
translated: When the famine had spread over the whole land, Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold grain to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe throughout Egypt.

ref: If we don't outsmart them, their families will keep growing larger. And if our country goes to war, they could easily fight on the side of our enemies and escape from Egypt.
text: Nyebaä, nyehãa woke̱ame̱ ayea ṅā mli, koni ame̱yi akafá ní ebaba mli ake, ké̱ ta ko bate ši le̱, ni ame̱ hũ ame̱yafata wo̱henye̱lo̱i le̱ ahe ní ame̱ke̱wo̱ ṅwu ní ame̱mīfã ke̱-ṅdše šikpoṅ le̱ no̱!
translated: Listen, I have told you this before, but if you do not take it to heart and continue doing what you want, and you disregard the advice given to you and do not change your ways, you will see the consequences for yourself!

ref: The king called them in again and asked, “Why are you letting those baby boys live?”
text: Ni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ tšé̱ yeife̱yei le̱ ni ekē̱ame̱ ake: Mẽhewo̱ nyefe nakai ni nyehere gbekẽbiihĩĩ le̱ ayiwala le̱?
translated: When the Egyptians say to you, ‘What is this sign you are showing us?’ what should I tell them?

ref: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Aaron ake̱: Ya ní oke̱ Mose ayakpe ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱. Ni ete ní eke̱le̱ yakpe ye̱ Nyoṅmo̱ goṅ le̱ he, ni eſo̱ enā.
translated: And Jehovah said to Aaron: Go and meet Moses in the wilderness. So he went and met him at the mountain of God, and kissed him.

ref: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
text: Ni Farao sā kē̱: Na, agbe̱ne̱ maṅ le̱ *yi efa momo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyehã ame̱dšo̱ö̱ amehe noṅṅ ne̱! *
translated: Then Pharaoh said: Well, let the people go and worship the Lord, but who and who are going?

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?
translated: When the Israelites saw that Pharaoh and his soldiers were coming after them, they asked: Why did you bring us out here?

ref: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
text: Ni Merari bii ne̱: Maheli ke̱ Muši. Mene̱mei dši Levi wekui le̱ ye̱ ame̱fo̱mo̱i le̱ anā.
translated: The sons of Merari were: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites according to their families.

ref: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
text: Mene̱mei dši Aaron ke̱ Mose ni Iehowa kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyeyadšiea Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ame̱tai le̱ anā le̱.
translated: The Lord said to Aaron and Moses: Bring the people of Israel out of Egypt by their divisions.

ref: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
text: ake̱ Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Midši Iehowa le̱; kē̱mo̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, nii fẽ ní mike̱o wieo̱ le̱.
translated: And Jehovah said to Moses: Go to Jehovah; for I have made Pharaoh, king of Egypt, stubborn, so that I may show my signs among them.

ref: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *
translated: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord killed all the firstborn in Egypt.
